events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need identify component/condition. Scenario: residential electrical terminations & splices, rough-complete, observed field condition: insufficient torque (thermal signature - IR modality). Reference NEC 110.14. Need decision fail because non-compliant/dangerous? Insufficient torque causes high resistance connections, overheating, fire risk. Component condition installed-defective. Need findings: loose connection, insufficient torque, abnormal thermal signature, potential overheating/fire hazard. Actions: torque terminations to manufacturer specs, inspect/replace damaged conductors/devices, verify with IR after retorque, test. References NEC 110.14. Need fill all fields. percent_complete maybe 80? value maybe 0 (no numeric reading). confidence high. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty, sound_source empty string. risk high or critical? Insufficient torque thermal signature indicates active overheating? In rough-complete not energized? But thermal signature observed via IR implies under load maybe high. Could be critical due to fire. But residential terminations insufficient torque is high risk. Let's decide high. component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Maybe also degraded if overheated. But visible defect cues: thermal hot spot, discoloration? The prompt says insufficient torque (thermal signature - IR modality). We'll include installed-defective and non-compliant. decision fail. rationale brief. Ensure valid JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Electrical termination/splice installed with insufficient torque",
    "Abnormal thermal signature detected by infrared inspection indicating high-resistance connection",
    "Overheating risk at conductor termination or splice",
    "Potential fire hazard due to loose connection"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize circuit before servicing",
    "Remove and inspect termination/splice and conductors for overheating damage",
    "Retorque all terminations to manufacturer specifications using a calibrated torque screwdriver or wrench",
    "Replace any damaged conductors, lugs, connectors, or devices",
    "Re-energize and verify corrected thermal performance with IR scan",
    "Document torque values and inspection results"
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 110.14 requires terminations to be tight and torqued per manufacturer specifications to ensure effective electrical contact and prevent overheating. An IR-detected thermal signature at a rough-complete termination indicates insufficient torque, creating a high-resistance, fire-risk condition.",
  "references": ["NEC 110.14"]
}
